title: Olivia Dean Trusted Herself, Burned Incense and Won a Grammy: Inside Her Journey to Best New Artist
text: Olivia Dean won Best New Artist at the 2026 Grammy Awards (February 1), defeating competitors including KATSEYE, The Marías, Addison Rae, sombr, Leon Thomas, Alex Warren, and Lola Young. The 26-year-old British neo-soul artist has been writing and releasing music for nearly a decade and wanted to pursue music since age 8. She attended The Brit School performing arts academy, alma mater of Amy Winehouse and Adele. Her sophomore album The Art of Loving drove her Grammy victory. The hit single Man I Need marked her first Billboard chart appearance and made her the first female solo artist to simultaneously have four tracks on the UK singles chart's Top 10. When recording The Art of Loving, she chose an East London rental to maintain creative control. Her family includes a British father who played Al Green and Carole King, and a Jamaican-Guyanese mother who introduced her to neo-soul artists. She credited her Grammy to being a product of bravery.
